meta.title: Variable Glossary
meta.boundary: Symbols are scoped per card. Repeated letters may mean different things in different formulas. This glossary gives common meanings only. These are symbolic design variables, not measurements.

operators.+: Stacks with; separate contributors add pressure, color, or meaning.
operators.×: Amplifies or compounds; variables intensify each other when paired.
operators./: Buffered by or softened by; denominator variables reduce, stabilize, contextualize, or repair the pattern.
operators.^2: Nonlinear spiral; the factor can run away with itself once intensified.
operators.3^R: Escalation through repeated rounds; each return grows the pattern.

roles.output: The named pattern or emotional/comic result produced by the symbolic equation.
roles.amplifier: A factor that increases pressure, intensity, charge, or narrative force.
roles.buffer: A factor that reduces, contextualizes, clarifies, or softens the pattern.
roles.stabilizer: A factor that helps the pattern become safer, clearer, or more usable.
roles.signal: A factor that points toward a need, boundary, or story truth.
roles.bridge: A factor that reconnects separated parts, people, or meanings.

designNotes: Symbols are local to each card, not globally fixed constants.
The same structure can produce different emotional payloads across families, especially Comedy and Drama.
These variables are meant for writing, game design, NPC design, reflection, and teaching prompts.
They should never be used to score, diagnose, shame, manipulate, or reduce a person.
